description: Canonical paper-arch list — single source of truth for which (arch_id, k_win) cells appear in the paper's leaderboard and T-sweep artefacts. Cell key is (subject_model, arch_id, seed, k_win); each leaderboard entry below is a (arch_id, k_win) pair, evaluated across both subject models and the seed priority below.

probing_methodology.comment: All paper cells must be probed under this methodology. 'old' methodology rows in the manifest (Phase 5/5B's last_position / mean_pool / full_window at S=20) do NOT qualify and must be re-probed.

training_constants.comment: All paper-cell ckpts must be trained with these constants — fixed across A40 / H200 / any other pod. Existing base-side Phase 7 ckpts on han1823123123/txcdr-base were trained at b=4096 on H200; lowering batch_size on any subset would create apples-to-oranges. If a cell can't fit on A40 at b=4096, DO NOT downsize batch — DEFER training to an H200 pod instead. Cross-pod numerical drift (cuDNN kernels) is acceptable; cross-batch-size drift is not.

training_pod_assignments.policy: Each (arch_id, k_win) gets a 'training_pod' tag: 'A40_ok' or 'H200_required'. A40_ok = fits comfortably on the A40 at b=4096 with the constants above (cache preload + W+Adam + workspace ≤ 42 GB, leaving 4 GB headroom). H200_required = exceeds A40 VRAM at b=4096; must wait for the H200 pod arriving in ~3 days.
training_pod_assignments.a40_vram_budget: 46 GB total, target ≤ 42 GB peak resident (4 GB headroom for CUDA fragmentation + activations during backward).
training_pod_assignments.h200_vram_budget: 141 GB — fits all paper-cell archs at b=4096.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: TFA uses inner tfa_batch_size=64 within the outer batch_size=4096; weights+attention scratch ~22 GB at most.
leaderboard_archs.comment: TFA full (predictive + novel codes) — Lubana et al. 2025 reference.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Per-token SAE; ~20 GB peak at b=4096.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Faithful T-SAE port (Ye et al. 2025) at the paper's native k=20 sparsity.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Per-token SAE; ~20 GB peak at b=4096.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Faithful T-SAE port at our k convention (k_win=500) for fair cross-arch comparison.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: preload_multilayer = 70.8 GB > 46 GB A40 VRAM. Even with cache off-GPU, the 5-layer cache exceeds the pod's 46 GB system-RAM cap (cgroup memory.limit_in_bytes). Disk-mmap streaming is the only A40 path; deferring to H200 instead avoids the refactor.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Dense MLC at Phase 7 k_win=500; per-layer label k_pos=100 is purely descriptive (single shared TopK).

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Same as paper_id mlc — multi-layer preload exceeds A40 VRAM and pod RAM. Note: Phase 5 IT ckpts at k_win=100 exist on han1823123123/txcdr but were trained at batch=1024 — they DO NOT count as paper-canonical at b=4096.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Sparse MLC at Phase 5 density (k_win=100). Trained from scratch at b=4096 on H200 to be apples-to-apples with the dense MLC paper cell.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Same multi-layer cache constraint as mlc.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Dense agentic MLC (multi-scale contrastive, n_scales=3, gamma=0.5) at k_win=500.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Same multi-layer cache constraint as mlc. Phase 5 IT ckpts at k_win=100 exist at b=1024 — not paper-canonical at b=4096.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Sparse agentic MLC at k_win=100 (Phase 5 density). Trained from scratch at b=4096 on H200.

leaderboard_archs.training_pod_rationale: Single-layer cache 14 GB + W+Adam 5 GB + workspace ≈ 22 GB.
leaderboard_archs.comment: Barebones TXC at T=5 (T-sweep peak point and one of the two T-sweep anchor archs).
Barebones TXC at T=16 (mid-T point; tests T-scaling claim at 3× the peak T).
Best TXC variant from Phase 5 — H8 stack (matryoshka H/L + multi-distance InfoNCE) + subseq sampling. mp/lp 3-seed champion in Phase 5.
Best Phase 7 single-cell at k_feat=20 (mean AUC 0.9358 on base). Barebones TXC + tsae-paper anti-dead stack only.
Best Phase 7 single-cell at k_feat=5 (mean AUC 0.8989 on base). H8 multidistance recipe = anti-dead + matryoshka H/L + multi-distance InfoNCE with auto-scaled shifts.

tsweep_T_cap.rationale: Both barebones and hill-climbed T-sweeps cap at T=32. Beyond this (e.g., row 49 phase5b_subseq_h8_T64_s5) is permanently infeasible on A40 — see 2026-04-28-a40-feasibility.md.

tsweep_barebones.comment: Existing Phase 7 T-sweep (preserved). All cells trained on base at seed=42 + seed=1; partial seed=2 for T=3..10. Carries forward unchanged.

training_pod_per_T.rationale: single-layer cache (14 GB) + W+Adam (T·d_in·d_sae·12B/param) at b=4096 + ~3 GB workspace. T=24 → 41 GB ✓; T=28 → 45 GB borderline (assigned to H200 to keep 4 GB A40 headroom); T=32 → 50 GB > 46 GB. Per the no-apples-to-oranges rule, batch_size stays 4096 — borderline cells defer to H200.

tsweep_barebones.results_source: experiments/phase7_unification/results/probing_results.jsonl — filter rows where arch_id matches 'txcdr_t<T>' for T in T_values AND S=32. results_manifest.json summarises coverage.

tsweep_hillclimbed.comment: H8 multidistance recipe: TXC + anti-dead + matryoshka H/L (H = 0.2·d_sae) + multi-distance InfoNCE. Family-mean #1 in Phase 7 leaderboard at k_feat=20 (tightest cluster, 0.0038 spread).

training_pod_per_T.rationale: H8 adds matryoshka H decoder (~10% extra over plain TXC) and a small multi-distance pair tensor. Footprint at T=16: 14 + 18 + 3 = 35 GB ✓; T=18: 14 + 20.2 + 3 = 37 GB borderline (defers to H200 for 4 GB headroom); T=32: 14 + 36 + 3 = 53 GB > 46 GB. Per the no-apples-to-oranges rule, batch_size stays 4096 — borderline cells defer to H200.

tsweep_hillclimbed.rationale_for_choice: Hill-climbed family selected as phase57_partB_h8_bare_multidistance because: (i) family-mean #1 in Phase 7 unified leaderboard at k_feat=20 (tightest cluster, 0.0038 spread); (ii) T=8 is paper good_txc_p7_k5 — the T-sweep gives that anchor full context; (iii) 7 of 16 cells already trained, only 9 missing — minimum new compute vs the txc_bare_antidead and SubseqH8 alternatives.

alternatives_rejected.txc_bare_antidead_t<T>: Only T=5,10,20 trained — would need 13 new trainings (full sweep). More compute, less differentiation from barebones (anti-dead only).
alternatives_rejected.phase5b_subseq_h8_T_max<T>: Only T_max=10,12 trained — would need 14 new trainings. T_max=64 permanently lost on A40 anyway.

tsweep_hillclimbed.results_source: experiments/phase7_unification/results/probing_results.jsonl — filter rows where arch_id matches 'phase57_partB_h8_bare_multidistance_t<T>' for T in T_values AND S=32. results_manifest.json summarises coverage.
